# Questions in Livestream 151

Live Stream #151: Nine Myths About Spam

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp. The links use HTML anchors so GitHub can render them with `target="_blank"`.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=616" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">10:16</a> | Could gods like Osiris or Rama be based on real people later deified? | He leans more toward historical people being mythologized than toward an Egyptian divine-council explanation, while leaving room for uncertainty. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=850" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">14:10</a> | What does the Mount Ebal cultic site add to the case for the Exodus? | He says it fits a late-conquest chronology well and is hard to dismiss as anything other than a cult site. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=1841" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">30:41</a> | What was the Egyptian title `kheret nesut`, and why did it emerge? | He describes it as a title for high-status court women or ladies in waiting, but says its exact origin and role are uncertain. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=2074" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">34:34</a> | What music do you listen to, if any? | He says his tastes are eclectic, including soundtracks, alternative music, anime music, and other styles. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=2489" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">41:29</a> | Does "spam" here mean the meat or Ron Wyatt fans? | He says the title joke means the meat. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=2602" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">43:22</a> | What are the flaws of purity culture? | He says purity culture had legitimate concerns but often handled sexuality in unhealthy or excessive ways. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=2986" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">49:46</a> | Did Egyptians need to be buried in Egyptian soil to reach the afterlife? | He says no; burial in foreign territory did not automatically block the afterlife. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=3187" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">53:07</a> | Can Christians celebrate nature, and what about non-demonic nature spirits? | He says nature can be celebrated but praise belongs to God, and any good spirit would redirect attention back to God rather than to itself. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=3583" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">59:43</a> | Is Mark 2:26 wrong when Jesus says Abiathar was high priest when David ate the showbread? | He says Abiathar was not high priest at the moment but became the major priestly figure associated with the episode and its aftermath. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=4063" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:07:43</a> | When in Ramesses II's reign would the Exodus occur, and why would Egypt not keep chasing Israel? | He places it roughly around the king's twenty-fifth regnal year and says Egypt was too reduced, especially in chariot strength, to chase them across Sinai. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=4485" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:14:45</a> | What is one of your guilty pleasures? | He says taking a day off, because running the channel leaves little real downtime. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=4628" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:17:08</a> | What are your general thoughts on Edgar Allan Poe? | He calls Poe an excellent writer with a strange life and death, and mentions liking his work. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=4683" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:18:03</a> | What are your thoughts on Jean-Paul Sartre and Ernest Hemingway? | He regards both as good writers, while noting Sartre's atheism and late-life conversion claims in the discussion. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=5121" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:25:21</a> | What are your thoughts on H. P. Lovecraft? | He says Lovecraft was very good at creating atmosphere and mood, which he sees as central to Lovecraft's fiction. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=6003" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:40:03</a> | What do you mean by the Torah as Moses's memoir? | He compares it to day-by-day court writing later compiled into a single work by an editor. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=6363" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:46:03</a> | How could Jephthah have escaped his vow? | He points to Leviticus 27 as a redemption provision for an extreme or hasty vow. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=6786" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:53:06</a> | How would Egyptians smack-talk or insult people? | He discusses Egyptian pejoratives and says some insults were humorous, but the transcript only gives a partial survey. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=7314" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:01:54</a> | How do we know Numbers 12:3 is not a later self-praising insertion? | He says the wording does not read like ordinary self-praise and fits the narrative style rather than a simple boast. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=8430" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:20:30</a> | How should 2 John 9-11 be understood? | He warns that the passage concerns rejecting false teaching and not supporting a teacher who does not remain in Christ's doctrine. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=9351" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:35:51</a> | Why distinguish between a christophany and a theophany? | He says the terms overlap but can be distinguished when identifying whether the appearance is specifically of Christ or more broadly of God. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=12660" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:31:00</a> | What are your thoughts on Denis Villeneuve's newer Dune films? | He likes Dune broadly but says he did not care for Part Two because of major story and timeline changes. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=13706" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:48:26</a> | What are your thoughts on 1 Timothy 2:12? | He says women teaching in church is not the issue as such; he frames the passage around authority, order, and men failing to serve. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=14611" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:03:31</a> | Why is Rehoboam's mother Naamah the Ammonite mentioned twice in 1 Kings 14? | He says the repetition draws attention to her Ammonite identity and its covenantal significance. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=14910" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:08:30</a> | Why did Eve add "or touch it" to the divine command in Genesis 3? | He says changing God's wording changes the meaning and can become the first step toward getting around the command. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=15494" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:18:14</a> | Was Neferneferuaten really Nefertiti? | He says we do not know; Nefertiti, Smenkhkare, or one of Nefertiti's daughters are possible, but the evidence is too limited. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=15562" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:19:22</a> | Was Ramesses II overrated as a pharaoh? | He says yes as a military leader, but praises him as a prolific builder and architect. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=15985" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:26:25</a> | How should Exodus 4:24-26 be understood? | He says the issue is Moses failing to have his son circumcised, so Zipporah's act resolves a covenant violation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=16686" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:38:06</a> | How should 1 Corinthians 11:5-10, especially verse 10, be understood? | He says the reference to angels may mean heavenly beings or church messengers, so the verse has more than one possible reading. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=17065" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:44:25</a> | Did Moses write the Genesis patriarch stories, and were they oral tradition or compiled sources? | He thinks Moses likely recorded much of the material, but says the mix of oral and written sources before Sinai is hard to know. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=17247" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:47:27</a> | Did Egyptians worship the sun, and why? | Yes. He says sun worship is understandable because the sun is obviously life-giving, dangerous, and overwhelmingly powerful. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=17669" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:54:29</a> | What overlooked fact about John the Baptist stands out? | He says John was a Nazirite and highlights the vow's restrictions on hair, ritual purity, and the fruit of the vine. |
